fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New York Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New York - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity new york, how to transfer physical therapist license to new york, physical therapist compact new york, new york physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: New York Office of the Professions — PT
board.mailing_address:
New York State Education Department
Office of the Professions
Physical Therapy Unit
89 Washington Avenue
Albany, NY 12234-1000

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in New York.

steps.title: Create a MY NYSED Account
steps.description: Register an account on the New York State Education Department (NYSED) website to begin the application process through the NYSED Online Application System.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fee
steps.description: Complete the 'Application for Licensure (Form 1)' through your MY NYSED Account in the Professional Licensing section of the NYSED Online Application System and pay the non-refundable $294 fee.

steps.title: Verify Education
steps.description: Have your professional educational institution send official transcripts directly to NYSED. Use Form 2 for New York programs or Form 2F for foreign-educated applicants.

steps.title: Pass the NPTE
steps.description: Register with the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) and pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) after receiving approval from NYSED.

steps.title: Complete Clinical Experience and Required Training
steps.description: Ensure completion of 12 weeks of supervised clinical experience (Form 4B), approved infection control training (Form 2Q), and child abuse identification and reporting training (Form 2A), submitting documentation to NYSED as required.

steps.title: Receive License
steps.description: Once all requirements are met and verified by the New York State Education Department, your Physical Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New York physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New York have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers physical therapist practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to New York?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in New York
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in New York, applicants must use the NYSED Online Application System after creating a MY NYSED Account.

official_links.registration_steps: Create a MY NYSED Account on the New York State Education Department website, then log in and navigate to the 'Professional Licensing' section to start your application.
